sprint: Sprint 41 -- V0.27.17 B-104 Step 1 server-side analytics authority (US-348/US-349 redo via architecture shift) + side fixes
backlogItem: Bug-fix sprint #12 in the V0.27 chain. THIRD cycle of the V0.27.7 US-326/US-328 false-pass class -- shipped through to V0.27.16 despite Marcus's I-040 discipline (synthetic-seam-mock passes do NOT count; real-drive round-trip + DB read-back is the gate). Argus's 2026-05-21 drill of V0.27.16 (drive 20 = 3,808 realtime rows x 16 params, ~9 min real engine running, sequencer poweroff, auto-boot, 8-poll bench observation) confirmed: US-348 server drive_summary computed fields STILL NULL (same NULL pattern as drives 12-19 pre-fix); US-349 Pi drive_statistics STILL zero rows ever. Argus's root cause hypothesis: DriveDetector's drive-end signal does not fire when drive is terminated by sequencer poweroff (no engine-off OBD signal before stack tears down); recorder is wired for FUTURE drive-end events but never catches THIS drive's actual end; subsequent boots don't retroactively process unfinished prior-boot drives. Argus: 'cannot recommend a third redo of the same form.' CIO directive 2026-05-21: advance B-104 (just filed 30 min prior) Step 1 architectural shift INSTEAD of writer redo. Pi = telemetry emitter; server = analytics authority. Server computes drive_summary + drive_statistics from raw realtime_data (3,808 rows for drive 20 already on disk); no Pi-side writer trigger required; bug class structurally impossible. Pi keeps drive boundary EVENT logging (drive_start / drive_end timestamps, etc.) for Pi-health/diagnostics -- these are event records, not derived analytics. Pi-side drive_statistics table RETIRED entirely (CIO ratification 2026-05-21). Plus 3 side fixes bundled per CIO ratification: US-345 maxTrailBytes guard fix; deploy-pi.sh daemon-reload + service restart gap (Argus's morning find); I-040 structural close (deploy-context drive simulator). Branched from sprint/sprint40-bugfixes-V0.27.16 tip @ 78c7c2d per chain-end-merge rule. PM Rule 10 design-gate DoD applies: server analytics writer is load-bearing for data pipeline -> specs/architecture.md update required in-sprint.
createdBy: Marcus (PM) Session 41 2026-05-21 per CIO ratification of B-104 Step 1 advance as Sprint 41 fix path. AskUserQuestion answers 2026-05-21: scope=hybrid (option 3) with Pi keeping event-log records for diagnostics + server as authority for derived analytics; Pi-side drive_statistics table retire entirely; backfill drives 12-20 in Sprint 41; Atlas briefed before scope-lock; all 3 side fixes bundled. Per-story acceptance + verification + gate criteria pre-registered by Atlas at Ralph dispatch (mirrors Sprint 39/40 cadence).

testBaseline.note: Inherited from V0.27.11 / Sprint 37 baseline (commit 6184a7f). Carried through V0.27.12-15 + V0.27.16. Re-snapshot after Sprint 41 lands -- B-104 Step 1 will touch server-side analytics surface meaningfully.

sprintNotes: THIRD CYCLE of the V0.27.7 US-326/US-328 false-pass class. Marcus + Atlas + Argus aligned: a third naive writer redo is not the answer. CIO directive: advance B-104 Step 1 architectural shift as the structural fix.

B-104 architectural principle: Pi = telemetry emitter, server = analytics authority. Pi may compute in-drive aggregates locally (engine on = AC power, no battery cost) but does NOT transmit derived analytics. Pi DOES log drive boundary events (drive_start / drive_end timestamps) for Pi-health/diagnostics -- these are event records, not derived analytics. Server is sole writer of persisted analytics tables.

Pi-side drive_statistics table RETIRED entirely (CIO 2026-05-21). No more Pi-side stats writer wiring. drive_summary Pi-side computed-field writer retired; Pi keeps event-log fields (drive_start_timestamp, ambient_temp_at_start_c, starting_battery_v, data_source) for diagnostics.

Server recompute trigger: overnight batch + on-demand recompute. Idempotent: same raw data + same logic = same output. Backfill of drives 11-20 (NULL drive_summary fields + missing drive_statistics rows; Drive 11 added per Spool FLAG-2 + Argus DB-state check 2026-05-21) runs the new server compute path as the empirical validation of the architecture.

B-100 (drive_summary writer broken / empty shells), US-326 (V0.27.7 server analytics NULL fix that false-passed), US-348 (V0.27.16 redo that false-passed), US-328 (V0.27.7 Pi-side writer schema-only Option C), US-349 (V0.27.16 redo that false-passed) all SUPERSEDED on Sprint 41 land.

I-040 structural close (US-355): deploy-context drive simulator. The 3rd-cycle bug class shipped because unit-test fixtures don't reproduce deploy-time runtime conditions. The structural fix is a test surface that drives the integrated orchestrator + DriveDetector + recorder + sync + server compute path against a real database, NOT a mock of any seam. Marcus + Atlas + Argus + Ralph design at PRD-grooming time.

Side fix US-353: US-345 maxTrailBytes guard fix. Per Argus's drill: first post-deploy reboot tripped maxTrailBytes=65536 because trail accumulated unbounded while F-8 was broken. F-8 design SOUND post-fix; guard interaction non-chain-blocking but worth fixing alongside the deploy-hygiene + B-104 work.

Side fix US-354: deploy-pi.sh daemon-reload + service restart gap. Argus's morning find: V0.27.16 deploy wrote files to disk + bumped .deploy-version but did NOT restart eclipse-powerwatch.service + did NOT run systemctl daemon-reload. Pi was running V0.27.15 code in memory despite .deploy-version reporting V0.27.16/5837239. Argus worked around with daemon-reload + reboot on bench before drill. Would have produced false-negative F-7/F-8 validation if not caught.

Per-task acceptance/gate criteria pre-registered by Atlas at Ralph dispatch time. PM writes the sprint contract (intent + scope + groundingRefs + invariants); Atlas writes the acceptance + verification + per-task gates. Sprint 39/40 cadence preserved.

Tester /sprint-validated for Sprint 40 NOT RUNNING per Argus 2026-05-21: US-348 + US-349 fail acceptance; chain merge HELD per Mike 2026-05-08/10 chain-end-merge rule. regression_manifest F-008/F-011/F-012 stays HELD. Sprint 41 IRL re-validation is the unblock trigger.

US-346 Atlas T3 §10.6 design-gate sign-off STILL PENDING from Sprint 40. Argus's drill report noted: 'haven't seen Atlas T3-PASS sign-off; need confirmation before /sprint-validated.' Independent of Sprint 41 work but blocks /sprint-validated decision.

Atlas's 2026-05-21 accuracy review (ACCURATE on load-bearing factual claims; 19/19 filesToTouch+filesToRead UPDATE paths verified EXIST; 5/5 NEW paths verified ABSENT) landed mid-spin. Finding 1 (substantive imprecision) -- US-354 filesToTouch wording: deploy-pi.sh ALREADY HAS daemon-reload+restart logic at step_install_power_watch_unit:936-991 + step_install_boot_progress_units; the actual bug is the $changed gate (TRUE only on unit-file diff; silently skips restart when only Python source changes). Atlas's tightening LANDED in US-354 intent + filesToTouch + invariants (decouple-restart-from-unit-file-diff; audit all $changed-gated restarts; pattern class = condition-gating defect, same tokenization as F-7 edge-only polling). Finding 2 (trivial 4-vs-5-flags cross-doc inconsistency) -- NON-ISSUE: c51065c is Spool's commit landing 5-flag Spool audit; sprint.json sprintNotes describing Argus's 4-flag audit is correct. Argus's 2026-05-21 Drive 11 DB-state check (per Spool FLAG-2 + CIO ratification): outcome (a) CONFIRMED. chi-srv-01 obd2db query results -- drive_summary row 15 (source_id=11) has start_time/end_time/duration_seconds NULL + row_count=0 + is_real=0 (identical NULL pattern to drives 12-19 pre-fix); drive_statistics has zero rows for drive_id=11 (identical zero pattern). US-352 scope WIDENED drives 12-20 -> drives 11-20 (10 drives total); bigDoD #3 row-count updated 9 -> 10 drives. Drive 11 (2026-05-09, 93 octane) is Spool's authoritative pre-mod knock-retard reference baseline anchored in knowledge.md; including it puts Spool's tuning baseline in one regime post-Sprint-41 (vs legacy + new). Argus's side observation -- Drive 11 row_count=0 reading is its own tiny smell (Pi reported 0 rows when Drive 11 actually has 10,839 realtime rows on Pi per Argus's 2026-05-12 validation; same NULL/zero shape as 12-19); server compute path will recompute row_count from realtime_data COUNT(*), so pre-fix row_count=0 is structurally moot post-US-350-fix. Argus DB-check file: offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-21-from-argus-ack-drive-11-db-state-confirmed-null-zero-widen-us-352.md.

Atlas's 2026-05-21 16:36 per-task gate pre-registration (offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-21-from-atlas-sprint41-per-task-gates-preregistered.md) -- ARCHITECTURAL VERDICT SOUND. Acceptance + verification arrays for all 7 stories US-350..US-356 transcribed into sprint.json. 7 brief design questions VERDICTED: Q1 overnight batch + on-demand BOTH land in V0.27.17 (one systemd timer fires both compute paths); Q2 boundary from realtime_data MIN/MAX as primary, Pi event-log as enrichment, is_real derived from data_source ('real'->1, 'simulator'->0, NULL->NULL not silent 0); Q3 idempotent DROP TABLE IF EXISTS + first-boot row-count logging + subsequent-boot absent-confirmation; Q4 server drive_statistics DDL specified (drive_id FK CASCADE, data_quality ENUM full/sparse/below_threshold, computed_at observable idempotency); Q5 minimum viable harness ships V0.27.17 w/ ONE scenario seeded (V0.27.16 false-pass reproducer), retroactive RED proof = load-bearing acceptance; Q6 NEW section 'Data Pipeline Architecture' in specs/architecture.md w/ honest empirical-gated language; Q7 V0.27.17 patch-bump within V0.27 chain confirmed. 3 Argus refinements DISPOSED: A generic invariants only V0.27.17 + per-PID envelopes deferred V0.28+; B graceful sparse-drive via data_quality column; C 3-reboot minimum + forced-large-trail conditional on <30min build cost. Atlas-recommended Ralph dispatch sequence: US-346 (Sprint 40 carry-forward) -> US-353 + US-354 (parallel small) -> US-350 + US-351 (parallel M+L) -> US-352 -> US-355 -> US-356.

stories.title: B-104 Step 1a: server-side drive_summary compute from raw (US-326+US-348 redo)
stories.intent: B-104 architectural shift Step 1a. Server reads raw realtime_data + Pi-synced event logs (drive_start_timestamp + ambient_temp_at_start_c + starting_battery_v + data_source) + computes drive_summary fields (start_time, end_time, duration_seconds, row_count, is_real, analytics) on a server-side trigger that does NOT depend on a Pi-side drive-end signal. Pi-side drive_summary computed-field writer retired; Pi keeps event-log fields for diagnostics per Spool data v2 contract. Argus's root cause (DriveDetector drive-end signal does not fire on sequencer-driven termination) becomes structurally moot -- server reads timestamps from realtime_data MIN/MAX, doesn't depend on a marker event. Trigger options for Atlas at dispatch: (a) on every sync batch receipt for any drive_id touched, (b) on detection of a drive's last realtime_data row being older than N minutes (soak window), (c) overnight batch + on-demand. CIO ratified overnight batch + on-demand. Diagnostic-first: pre-flight rg sweep of existing US-326/US-348 server writer surface; document what is being retired vs. preserved.

filesToTouch: src/server/services/analysis.py (UPDATE -- retire the connection_log + drive_summary-payload trigger seam from US-348; new server-side compute path: read raw realtime_data + Pi event-log fields, compute analytics fields, persist; modification history updated with US-350 / B-104 reference)

src/server/api/sync.py (UPDATE -- _tryAutoAnalysisTrigger retired or reframed; server compute no longer fires from sync receipt seam in the V0.27.7-V0.27.16 form; Atlas finalizes trigger architecture at dispatch)

drive_statistics work (US-351 owns)
regression_manifest.json (Tester's lane)
B-104 Step 2+ scope (GEM family, Mahalanobis, etc. -- V0.28+)

groundingRefs.value: Argus drill 2026-05-21: drive 20 = 3,808 realtime_data rows x 16 parameter_names already on server post-sync; drive_summary row 27 has start_time / end_time / duration_seconds / row_count / is_real all NULL. Same NULL pattern across drives 12-19. Server-side compute path CAN compute the right values from realtime_data MIN/MAX + COUNT -- the raw data is canonical and present.

Argus root cause hypothesis: DriveDetector's drive-end signal does not fire when drive is terminated by sequencer poweroff (no engine-off OBD signal before stack tears down). Server compute path that reads raw realtime_data + timestamps does NOT depend on a Pi-side drive-end marker -- structural fix.

B-104 architectural principle (CIO 2026-05-21): Pi = emitter, server = analytics authority. Pi keeps event-log records (drive_start / drive_end timestamps) for diagnostics; server computes derived analytics from raw event stream. Idempotent overnight batch + on-demand recompute.

acceptance: Server has callable compute path compute_drive_summary(drive_id) in src/server/analytics/drive_summary_compute.py. Reads realtime_data rows for drive_id; computes start_time = MIN(timestamp_utc), end_time = MAX(timestamp_utc), duration_seconds = end_time - start_time, row_count = COUNT(*); enriches with Pi event-log fields (ambient_temp_at_start_c, starting_battery_v, data_source) where available; derives is_real from data_source ('real' -> 1, 'simulator' -> 0, NULL -> NULL not silently 0); UPSERTs idempotently into drive_summary.

Trigger seam retired: _tryAutoAnalysisTrigger at src/server/api/sync.py:721 is DELETED (preferred) OR raises NotImplementedError tripwire if anyone tries to re-introduce the path. No path from sync receipt to compute.

Overnight batch lands: deploy/server-analytics-batch.service + deploy/server-analytics-batch.timer NEW; timer fires daily; service runs compute over all drive_ids with NULL drive_summary computed fields; deploy/deploy-server.sh installs them sync-if-changed + daemon-reload + enable-if-not-already.
On-demand CLI: python -m server.cli.recompute_drive_analytics --drive-id N invokes compute for a single drive; --drive-id-range 11-20 for range; --all-stale for all drives with NULL fields.
Idempotent: compute_drive_summary(N) invoked twice produces identical drive_summary row values (computed_at may update; data values identical).
Gap-detection tripwire: for each drive_id, realtime_data rows share contiguous timestamps; gaps >5 min logged at WARN-level (defensive, not failing the compute).
Pi-side src/pi/obdii/drive_summary.py event-log fields preserved (drive_start_timestamp, ambient_temp_at_start_c, starting_battery_v, data_source); computed-field writer code paths retired.
Raw realtime_data table + sync transport UNTOUCHED.
verification: python -c "from server.analytics.drive_summary_compute import compute_drive_summary" -- module importable
pytest tests/server/analytics/test_drive_summary_compute.py -v -- unit test fixture-based compute against synthetic realtime_data
pytest tests/server/analytics/test_drive_summary_compute.py::test_idempotent -v -- re-run produces identical output
pytest tests/server/analytics/test_drive_summary_compute.py::test_is_real_derivation -v -- data_source='real' -> 1; 'simulator' -> 0; NULL -> NULL (not silently 0)
pytest tests/server/analytics/test_drive_summary_compute.py::test_gap_detection_warns -v -- synthetic realtime_data with >5-min gap produces WARN log, not failure
grep -n '_tryAutoAnalysisTrigger\|auto_analysis' src/server/api/sync.py -- no live calls (only modification history references OR explicit NotImplementedError tripwire)
grep -n 'drive_summary' src/pi/obdii/drive_summary.py -- only event-log writer surface; no computed-field code paths
test -f deploy/server-analytics-batch.service && test -f deploy/server-analytics-batch.timer -- systemd timer files exist
python -m server.cli.recompute_drive_analytics --help -- CLI runnable
git diff sprint/sprint40-bugfixes-V0.27.16..HEAD -- src/pi/obdii/realtime_data.py src/pi/obdii/sync/ -- empty (or only modification-history comments)

stories.title: B-104 Step 1b: server drive_statistics compute + retire Pi table (US-328+US-349 redo)
stories.pmSignOff: Marcus (PM) 2026-05-21 -- size L approved: 8 filesToTouch spans Pi-side retirement (4 files: drive_statistics.py DELETE, database_schema.py UPDATE, drive/detector.py UPDATE, orchestrator/lifecycle.py UPDATE) + Pi-side test retirement (2 files: test_drive_statistics_writer.py DELETE, test_drive_statistics_pi_table_migration.py UPDATE-OR-DELETE) + new server-side surface (2 files: db/models.py UPDATE, analytics/drive_statistics_compute.py NEW). Retirement-plus-build symmetry is genuinely L-sized. CIO 2026-05-21 ratified Pi-side complete retirement; B-104 Step 1b lands the architectural shift; cannot meaningfully sub-divide without leaving the system in a half-migrated state mid-sprint.
stories.intent: B-104 architectural shift Step 1b. Server reads raw realtime_data + computes per-parameter_name stats (min / max / avg / std_dev / outlier_min / outlier_max) + writes to server-side drive_statistics table. Pi-side drive_statistics table RETIRED entirely (CIO 2026-05-21 ratified) -- migration drops it; DriveStatisticsRecorder removed from src/pi/obdii/drive_statistics.py and orchestrator wiring; tests retired. The V0.27.16 US-349 wiring (DriveDetector._endDrive -> recorder seam) goes away. The Pi-side schema declaration in database_schema.py + ALL_SCHEMAS registration goes away. Subsequent boots will not migrate the table; existing Pi rows (zero, per Argus) are not synced anywhere. Server-side trigger: overnight batch + on-demand (same path as US-350). Diagnostic-first: pre-flight rg sweep of all Pi-side drive_statistics surface to confirm complete retirement.

groundingRefs.value: Argus drill 2026-05-21: Pi drive_statistics has zero rows total / zero distinct drives after V0.27.16 deploy + drive 20 round-trip + 8-poll bench observation. DriveStatisticsRecorder wired per init log but never wrote a row for drive 20 (which has 3,808 realtime rows x 16 params ready to aggregate). Server-side compute path reads same raw data + does not depend on Pi-side drive-end signal.
CIO 2026-05-21 ratification: Pi-side drive_statistics table retire entirely -- server-side only. No local-only-in-drive copy needed for dashboard/alerts.

acceptance: Pi-side retirement complete: src/pi/obdii/drive_statistics.py DELETED; SCHEMA_DRIVE_STATISTICS + ALL_SCHEMAS registration REMOVED from src/pi/obdii/database_schema.py; idempotent migration added (DROP TABLE IF EXISTS) running on first Pi boot post-V0.27.17 deploy with first-boot row-count logging 'INFO eclipse-obd | drive_statistics table dropped (was: N rows / M distinct drives)' + subsequent-boot 'DEBUG | drive_statistics table absent (retired V0.27.17 per US-351)'; driveStatisticsRecorder kwarg + setter + _recordDriveStatistics REMOVED from src/pi/obdii/drive/detector.py; _initializeDriveStatisticsRecorder + init call REMOVED from src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/lifecycle.py; tests/pi/obdii/test_drive_statistics_writer.py DELETED; tests/pi/obdii/test_drive_statistics_pi_table_migration.py REPURPOSED as migration-drop test.

Server-side build complete: src/server/db/models.py adds (or amends to) drive_statistics schema per Atlas Q4 DDL -- drive_id INT NOT NULL FK drive_summary.id ON DELETE CASCADE, parameter_name VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL, min_value/max_value/avg_value/std_dev/outlier_min/outlier_max DOUBLE, sample_count INT NOT NULL, data_quality ENUM('full','sparse','below_threshold') NOT NULL DEFAULT 'full', computed_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, PRIMARY KEY (drive_id, parameter_name), INDEX idx_drive_statistics_quality.
src/server/analytics/drive_statistics_compute.py NEW: callable compute_drive_statistics(drive_id) reads realtime_data, groups by parameter_name, computes stats, classifies data_quality, UPSERTs.
Generic invariants RAISE if violated: min_value <= avg_value <= max_value, std_dev >= 0, no NaN/inf, sample_count >= 1 (Atlas Refinement A; per-PID envelopes deferred V0.28+).
data_quality classification: 'below_threshold' if sample_count <10; 'sparse' if 10-99; 'full' if >=100 (Atlas Refinement B).

stories.title: Side fix: US-345 maxTrailBytes guard fix
stories.intent: Per Argus's drill 2026-05-21: first post-deploy reboot tripped maxTrailBytes=65536 guard on boot-progress trail. Trail accumulated unbounded while F-8 was broken (every shutdown classified crashed_during_operation -> trail never trimmed at CLEAN_COMPLETE). F-8 design SOUND post-US-345 fix; once trail trims on clean shutdowns the unbounded growth stops. Guard interaction is the issue: a one-time post-deploy trail-size cleanup OR a more permissive maxTrailBytes value OR a guard that auto-trims rather than refusing-to-write. Atlas + Ralph finalize the fix shape at dispatch. Argus's filed finding at tester/findings/2026-05-21-us-345-f8-fix-blocked-by-maxtrailbytes-guard.md has the technical detail (Argus-lane file; PM does not read directly per lane discipline; Atlas mediates if cross-lane reference needed). Non-chain-blocking; rides V0.27.17 alongside the B-104 work.

filesToTouch: src/pi/diagnostics/boot_progress.py (UPDATE -- maxTrailBytes handling; Atlas finalizes fix shape at dispatch: auto-trim vs. higher cap vs. one-time cleanup)
tests/pi/diagnostics/test_boot_progress.py (UPDATE-IF-NEEDED -- coverage for new maxTrailBytes behavior; create if file does not exist)
filesToRead: deploy/boot-progress-finalize.service (post-US-345 fix state -- the F-8 fix)
offices/architect/inbox/ (Atlas relays Argus's finding if needed; PM does not read tester/findings/ per lane discipline)
doNotTouch: F-8 fix (Conflicts=shutdown.target directive -- US-345 lands correctly; do not regress)
boot_progress writer code path (CLEAN_COMPLETE emission is correct)
startup_log schema

groundingRefs.value: Argus drill 2026-05-21: first post-deploy reboot tripped maxTrailBytes=65536. F-8 design sound; guard interaction is the issue. Subsequent 4 reboots all passed once trail trimmed.
groundingRefs.source: Argus drill report 2026-05-21 (PM inbox) + Argus finding (tester/findings/, Atlas mediates if cross-lane reference needed)

acceptance: Fix shape: auto-trim with WARN log emission on write (Atlas-preferred over refuse-to-write or unconditional cap-raise). On write: if current trail size + new content > maxTrailBytes, trim oldest entries to fit; emit WARN log 'boot_progress trail trimmed: was <X> bytes, trimmed to <Y> bytes, current write would have exceeded maxTrailBytes=<Z>'. Don't refuse the write (Argus's V0.27.16 drill: refuse-to-write blocks boot progress logging entirely -> observability hole). Don't silently truncate without logging (silent failure mode). Ralph may challenge with empirically simpler shape via flag-don't-improvise.
F-8 fix preserved: boot-progress-finalize.service Conflicts=shutdown.target directive UNCHANGED.
boot_progress writer code path: CLEAN_COMPLETE emission unchanged.
startup_log schema unchanged.

3-reboot harness test: 3 consecutive simulated clean shutdowns + reboots produce 3 consecutive prior_boot_clean=1 writes + no guard trip across all 3 cycles. Trail size after each cycle stays bounded (<maxTrailBytes).

Forced-large-trail harness (CONDITIONAL on cost): if Ralph estimates <30 min to build, include -- artificially pre-seed trail to ~95% maxTrailBytes; reboot; confirm auto-trim fires + WARN log emitted + boot progresses normally. Otherwise defer to V0.28+.

pytest tests/pi/diagnostics/test_boot_progress.py::test_three_consecutive_reboots_no_trip -v -- 3 consecutive simulated reboots pass

stories.title: Side fix: deploy-pi.sh daemon-reload + service restart gap
stories.intent: Per Argus's morning find 2026-05-21: V0.27.16 deploy wrote files to disk + bumped .deploy-version but eclipse-powerwatch.service did NOT restart + daemon-reload did NOT run. Pi was running V0.27.15 code in memory despite .deploy-version reporting V0.27.16/5837239. Argus worked around with bench daemon-reload + reboot before drill. Would have produced false-negative F-7/F-8 validation if not caught. Per Atlas 2026-05-21 accuracy review: deploy-pi.sh ALREADY HAS daemon-reload + restart logic (step_install_power_watch_unit:936-991 and step_install_boot_progress_units), but the $changed gate is set TRUE only when the UNIT FILE differs (cmp -s); when only Python source changes -- the common code-deploy case -- $changed=false and restart is silently skipped. Same anti-pattern in step_install_boot_progress_units. Fix: decouple service restart from unit-file-change gate; ensure eclipse-powerwatch + eclipse-obd restart on every code-or-unit deploy + audit all $changed-gated restarts + add PID-start-time verification post-restart pre-.deploy-version-bump. Pattern class: condition-gating defect (narrower predicate silently absorbing a broader case) -- same tokenization as F-7's edge-only post-grace check. Non-chain-blocking once worked around but every Pi deploy ships dead code until manual reboot.

filesToTouch: deploy/deploy-pi.sh (UPDATE -- decouple service restart from unit-file-change gate; ensure eclipse-powerwatch + eclipse-obd restart on every code-or-unit deploy, not only on unit-file diff. Audit all $changed-gated restarts (step_install_power_watch_unit:944-988 + step_install_boot_progress_units:917-924 + likely sibling sections). Add PID-start-time verification post-restart, pre-.deploy-version-bump, that both services show STARTED later than deploy start. Per Atlas 2026-05-21 accuracy review Finding 1: this is a condition-gating bug class (narrower predicate silently absorbing a broader case -- same tokenization as F-7's edge-only polling).)
tests/deploy/test_deploy_pi.py (UPDATE -- static check that service restart fires on Python-source-only diffs (not just unit-file diffs); PID-verification step is present)

Systemd unit file contents (US-345 etc. own those)

groundingRefs.value: Argus's morning find 2026-05-21: V0.27.16 deploy didn't restart eclipse-powerwatch.service or daemon-reload. Pi ran V0.27.15 code in memory despite .deploy-version=V0.27.16/5837239. ps -o pid,lstart,cmd -p 734 showed STARTED Wed May 20 20:35:41 -- service untouched since pre-deploy boot.

acceptance: Decouple restart from unit-file-change gate in deploy/deploy-pi.sh: step_install_power_watch_unit (lines 936-991) sudo systemctl restart eclipse-powerwatch.service invoked on EVERY deploy (not only when $changed=true); daemon-reload remains gated on $changed (daemon-reload is unit-file metadata reload; restart is process replacement -- different signals). Same fix applied to all $changed-gated restarts for long-running services. Audit: step_install_power_watch_unit, step_install_boot_progress_units (Type=oneshot -- different rules), step_install_drain_forensics_units, step_install_orphan_cleanup_units, step_restart_service (final eclipse-obd restart). Ralph + Atlas audit at dispatch which services need unconditional restart vs which are oneshot.

PID-start-time verification step added post-restart, pre-.deploy-version-bump: ps -o pid,lstart,cmd -p $(pidof -s eclipse-powerwatch) shows STARTED time later than deploy start timestamp; same check for eclipse-obd; if either fails deploy ABORTS with clear error (exit non-zero) and .deploy-version is NOT bumped on incomplete restart.
Idempotent on re-run: restart safe to invoke multiple times (systemd handles this natively).
deploy-server.sh UNTOUCHED (server-side deploy healthy per Argus's V0.27.16 drill -- eclipse-obd-server restart on chi-srv-01 already exhibits the desired restart-on-deploy behavior).
.deploy-version bump logic preserved; just relocated to AFTER restart-verification of both services.

stories.title: I-040 structural close: deploy-context drive simulator test surface
stories.intent: Structural close on the V0.27.7-V0.27.16 false-pass class (Tester I-040). Argus's analysis 2026-05-21: 'I cannot recommend a third redo of the same form.' Root cause of the recurrence: unit-test fixtures don't faithfully reproduce deploy-time runtime conditions (orchestrator initialization order; DriveDetector's drive-end signal vs. sequencer-driven termination; sync sweep cadence; trigger seam vs. real-data shape). The structural fix is a deploy-context test surface -- a 'drive simulator' that exercises the integrated orchestrator + DriveDetector + recorder + sync + server compute path against a real database (Pi-side SQLite + server-side MariaDB), driving from the same deploy artifact the IRL Pi runs. Marcus + Atlas + Argus + Ralph design at PRD-grooming time. This story may produce a test harness + a small number of seed scenarios; subsequent stories add scenario coverage. Non-chain-blocking but high-value for closing the discipline gap that allowed 3 cycles of the same bug to ship.

stories.title: PM Rule 10 design-gate -- specs/architecture.md amendment for B-104 Step 1
stories.intent: Apply PM Rule 10 design-gate DoD (CIO-approved 2026-05-18; PM administers, Atlas owns the gate). specs/architecture.md amendment in-sprint documenting the B-104 Step 1 data-pipeline architecture shift: (a) Pi = telemetry emitter + event-log writer; (b) server = sole authority for derived analytics (drive_summary + drive_statistics computed from raw realtime_data + Pi event logs); (c) Pi-side drive_statistics table retirement scope; (d) trigger seam shift (server compute does NOT depend on Pi-side drive-end signal); (e) idempotent recompute principle (overnight batch + on-demand; same raw + same logic = same output); (f) what's retired: V0.27.7 US-326 connection_log trigger seam + US-328 Option C schema-only + V0.27.16 US-348 dual-seam approach + US-349 Pi-side writer wiring. Filed by Argus's 2026-05-21 audit (flag #2): backlogItem invokes Rule 10 but zero stories owned it. This story closes the gap. Section number TBD; Atlas finalizes at dispatch (likely a new section or sub-section under data-pipeline architecture, paralleling Sprint 40 US-346 §10.6 pattern).

filesToTouch: specs/architecture.md (UPDATE -- add B-104 Step 1 data-pipeline architecture section per Atlas's section choice at dispatch; document Pi=emitter / server=authority cut + Pi-side drive_statistics retirement + trigger seam shift + idempotent recompute principle; update modification history)
doNotTouch: Sprint 40 US-346 §10.6 amendment (preserve; this stacks on it but documents a different architectural cut)
Other architecture.md sections (only the B-104-Step-1 section is in-scope per Rule 10)

groundingRefs.value: PM Rule 10 design-gate DoD: any sprint touching a load-bearing subsystem updates that subsystem's specs/architecture.md section in the same sprint (DoD, not follow-up). Server analytics writer + Pi-side data-pipeline retirement is load-bearing.

Argus 2026-05-21 sprint.json standards audit flag #2: backlogItem explicitly invokes Rule 10 ('server analytics writer is load-bearing for data pipeline -> specs/architecture.md update required in-sprint') but ZERO of the original 6 stories listed specs/architecture.md in scope.filesToTouch. Atlas will likely BLOCK at design-gate pass.

acceptance: NEW section in specs/architecture.md documenting B-104 Step 1 data-pipeline architecture (section number TBD pending TOC read at dispatch -- likely 'Data Pipeline Architecture' paralleling section 10.6 Shutdown Sequencer + section 11 EEPROM Wake Contract; if existing TOC has a Data Pipeline section, AMEND it).
Content (a) Architectural principle: Pi = telemetry emitter + event-log writer; server = sole authority for derived analytics.

Content (b) Compute path: server reads raw realtime_data + Pi event logs; computes drive_summary (boundary from realtime_data MIN/MAX; enrichment from Pi event-log fields) + drive_statistics (per-parameter aggregates with generic invariants + data_quality classification).

Content (c) Pi-side retirement scope: drive_statistics table dropped; drive_summary computed-field writer retired; Pi keeps event-log fields (drive_start_timestamp, ambient_temp_at_start_c, starting_battery_v, data_source) for diagnostics.
Content (d) Trigger seam shift: server compute does NOT depend on Pi-side drive-end signal; overnight batch + on-demand CLI; sync receipt is decoupled from compute.
Content (e) Idempotent recompute principle: same raw + same logic = same output; observable via computed_at timestamp; re-run safe.

Content (f) What's retired (with commit cross-links): V0.27.7 US-326 connection_log trigger seam (commit SHA); V0.27.7 US-328 Option C schema-only (commit SHA); V0.27.16 US-348 dual-seam (commit SHA); V0.27.16 US-349 Pi-side writer wiring (commit SHA). Cross-link to git log for archival traceability.

Sprint 40 US-346 section 10.6 amendment PRESERVED (no edits to section 10.6; B-104 section is additive). Other architecture.md sections UNCHANGED. Modification history updated with US-356 / Sprint 41 reference. NO code changes (US-350+US-351+US-352+US-355 own code; US-356 is spec-only).

Honest empirical-gated language: Sprint 39 T9 precedent. Cite drill evidence + IRL acceptance status + drilldown to commits. Don't certify beyond evidence -- e.g., 'backfill of drives 11-20 PASSED on <date> per Argus drill evidence' not 'backfill is reliable in all conditions'. If V0.27.17 IRL drill hasn't happened yet at the time US-356 is written, language should say 'IRL drill pending' not assert success preemptively.
Atlas Rule-10 sign-off granted after independent re-read of the amendment against the current code state (post US-350+US-351 land). This is the gate; Marcus closes sprint after.

bigDefinitionOfDone: US-350 IRL: deploy V0.27.17 -> CIO does a real drive -> server compute path runs (overnight batch OR on-demand invocation) -> drive_summary row for that drive has start_time / end_time / duration_seconds / row_count / is_real NON-NULL + arithmetically consistent with realtime_data MIN/MAX/COUNT

US-351 IRL: deploy V0.27.17 -> CIO does a real drive -> server compute path runs -> server-side drive_statistics has >=1 row per parameter_name present in realtime_data with sensible min/max/avg values; Pi-side drive_statistics table NOT PRESENT post-migration (idempotent drop confirmed)
US-352 IRL: post-deploy on-demand recompute over drives 11-20 produces drive_summary NON-NULL computed fields + drive_statistics rows for all 10 drives; idempotent re-run produces zero diff
US-353 IRL: post-deploy reboot does NOT trip maxTrailBytes guard (trail trims correctly on clean shutdowns post-F-8 fix; guard interaction resolved)

US-354 IRL: post-deploy verification -- ps -o pid,lstart,cmd -p $(pidof -s eclipse-powerwatch) AND $(pidof -s eclipse-obd) both show STARTED time later than deploy start; deploy log shows daemon-reload + service restart sequence for BOTH services; .deploy-version bump occurs AFTER restart-verification of both

US-355: harness applied to the V0.27.7 OR V0.27.16 deployed code MUST produce RED for the US-326 / US-348 (drive_summary NULL fields) and US-328 / US-349 (drive_statistics zero rows) conditions -- empirical proof the harness would have caught the 3-cycle false-pass class. Same harness applied to V0.27.17 post-Sprint-41-land produces GREEN for the same scenarios. Atlas + Argus + Ralph + Marcus sign-off on the harness design + the V0.27.7/V0.27.16 RED scenario coverage.
